## Highlights

- **`/ginee-update` actually works again.** It was broken on every standard install since v0.10.0 — the skill exited with `framework not found` because it looked for installer scripts that the bootstrap intentionally prunes. Fixed. One-time recovery for stuck installs is below. (D27)
- **Cheaper LLM bills.** Before, every dispatch ran on whichever single model you'd picked in your client — usually the most expensive one, charged at the most expensive price for both architectural calls *and* sticky-comment updates. Now reasoning-heavy roles (orchestrator, architect) get the capable model and execution-heavy roles (engineers, tests, mechanical ops) get cheaper ones. Out of the box; nothing to configure. Per-task `model:reasoning` / `model:standard` / `model:fast` prefix overrides for one dispatch. (D31)
- **The team stops reinventing the wheel.** When picking up work that needs a new library, tool, or framework, the team used to default to building something custom — nothing forced it to look outward first. Now every Phase 2 design must surface real candidates (with versions and licenses) or explicitly state "I looked and nothing fit." (D30)
- **More room in your context window.** Subagent returns were burning context with narrative restatements, restated dispatch context, and embedded code dumps — measured at 1,500–15,000 chars per dispatch. Returns are now ~70% smaller on average (measured 3,603 → 1,136 chars on a real Phase-4 return). Faster turnaround, lower costs, more room for actual work. (D29)
- **More predictable skill commands.** Skills like `/ginee-pick-up` used to drift into orchestration on long sessions — drafting plans in the main thread, picking defaults silently, answering routing questions by reading bindings directly. Now skills are strictly mechanical; every judgment call hands off to `@team-lead`. Fewer "wait, why did it just decide that?" moments. (D28)

## Adopter migration

**Nothing to do** for D28, D29, D30, D31 — they're additive. The behaviour changes apply on your next dispatch.

**One-time recovery for D27** if you're stuck on v0.10.0 / v0.11.0 with a broken `/ginee-update`:

```powershell
$env:GINEE_UPDATE_ONLY='1'; $env:GINEE_ADAPTER='claude'; iwr -useb https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/main/install.ps1 | iex
```

```bash
GINEE_UPDATE_ONLY=1 GINEE_ADAPTER=claude bash -c "$(curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/main/install.sh)"
```

After that one-liner lands v0.12.0, every future update flows through `/ginee-update`.

Full spec for each change: [D31](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/blob/main/core/MIGRATIONS/D31-model-tier.md) · [D30](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/blob/main/core/MIGRATIONS/D30-adopt-existing-solution.md) · [D29](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/blob/main/core/MIGRATIONS/D29-strict-subagent-return-schema.md) · [D28](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/blob/main/core/MIGRATIONS/D28-skill-runner-boundary.md) · [D27](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/blob/main/core/MIGRATIONS/D27-installer-fetch-on-update.md).

**Full Changelog:** https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/compare/v0.11.0...v0.12.0
